a:r.a:t: After averaging ~$3.3B pre-OBBBA, demand peaks at ~$6.2B in FY26 and is expected to remain elevated at ~$5.1-5.8B per year through FY31.
Columbia funds biennially (FY24/26 in window). Virginia outsources ~24%, Columbia ~13%.
FYDP gross holds ~$19–21B a year (1 Columbia + 2 Virginias from 2028); capacity requires “strategically outsourcing workload to qualified suppliers” (PB27 SCN, Columbia P-10).
Outsourced 11–15%; FY2026 reads 34% on the OBBBA two-ship add plus EOQ AP/LLTM.
FYDP gross $3.9–6.3B a year (two-ship buys FY30–31); upper bound reflects stated intent to grow outsourced manhours ~30%.
Measured per FY and per class: outsourced BC TAM / total ship spend (constant FY2026 $).
Lower bound = FY22–25 class average; upper bound = lower x 1.30 (stated outsourcing intent).

Sources: Navy SCN P-5c / P-40 budget justification, FY22–FY27, and PB27 FYDP outyears (FY28–FY31); FY26, PL 119-21 Sec. 20002; PB27 SCN, Columbia P-10 Strategic Outsourcing narrative; OUSD(C) Green Book Procurement deflators; Navy Shipbuilding Plan
